issues.description: create_solution_map is a functionally identical duplicate of the existing build_solved_values (lines 55-74). Both assert equal lengths, zip params with f64 values, map each pair to (id.clone(), Value::Scalar { si_value, dimension: dimension_of(&param_type) }), and collect into HashMap<ValueCellId, Value>. The only differences are parameter names (params/x vs specifications/solution_values) and doc comments. build_solved_values is already called in three places (lines 537, 662, 697) and has four dedicated tests (lines 2335-2464).
issues.suggested_fix: Do not add create_solution_map. Use the existing build_solved_values instead. If clearer parameter names are desired, rename the parameters of build_solved_values directly.

issues.description: create_solution_map has zero callers anywhere in the codebase. Adding an unused private function bloats the module and will trigger a dead_code warning from rustc/clippy.
issues.suggested_fix: Either remove the function entirely or replace it with a call site that actually uses it. If this is intended for future use, add it when the call site is introduced.

issues.description: No tests are added for create_solution_map, while the identical build_solved_values has four dedicated tests. If this function were to stay, it would need equivalent test coverage, but since it's a duplicate that shouldn't exist, the existing tests on build_solved_values already cover the behavior.
issues.suggested_fix: Remove the duplicate function; existing build_solved_values tests already cover this logic.

comprehensive_reviewer.summary: The diff introduces create_solution_map, which is a line-for-line semantic duplicate of the existing build_solved_values function (same assertion, same zip-map-collect pattern, same Value::Scalar construction, same return type). The only differences are parameter names and doc wording. The new function has zero callers anywhere in the codebase, so it is dead code that will trigger a compiler warning and adds maintenance burden. This should be rejected: callers should use the existing build_solved_values, or if better naming is desired, rename its parameters in place.
